item.title: Morgan Stanley Among Banks Challenging Growing Dollar Optimism
item.description: Currency strategists at Credit Agricole, Morgan Stanley and TD Securities are among those bucking the consensus call for a stronger dollar.

item.title: SpaceX Analyst Debut Set to Test $2.2 Trillion Valuation
item.description: Investors in SpaceX have been largely flying blind since the company’s record-breaking IPO last month, with few financial projections to help determine what the stock is actually worth.

item.title: SBI Funds’ $1.2 Billion India IPO Launch in July to Test Demand
item.description: SBI Funds Management Ltd. is set to launch its initial public offering, which could raise up to $1.2 billion, in the week starting July 13, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand ahead of a busy pipeline of billion-dollar listings in India.

item.title: What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China
item.description: China's great economic contradiction.

item.title: What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China
item.description: What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China【原文：What Dan Wang Saw on His Last Trip to China】

item.title: Japan Retail Investors Bought Record Stocks Last Week in Selloff
item.description: Japan’s retail investors purchased a record amount of local shares last week, when a tech selloff sent equity gauges plunging.

item.title: SAP Restricts Hiring, Travel to Fund ‘Significant’ AI Push
item.description: SAP SE said it will cut back hiring and travel to save costs as Europe’s largest software company devotes more resources to developing artificial intelligence technologies and fending off new competitors.

item.title: Google Loses EU Top Court Fight Over €4.1 Billion Android Fine
item.description: Google lost its long-running fight against a €4.1 billion ($4.7 billion) European Union antitrust fine after the bloc’s top judges said regulators were right to punish the US giant for abusing Android’s market power.

item.title: SoftBank Plans AI Cloud Services in US to Tap Surging Demand
item.description: SoftBank Group Corp. and its telecom unit will start renting AI computing resources to US companies next fiscal year in a bid to capitalize on the company’s growing pipeline of data center projects.

item.title: Quantum Systems More Than Doubles Valuation to $8 Billion
item.description: Quantum Systems has raised $1.2 billion in a funding round, more than doubling the German drone startup’s valuation to about $8 billion post-money, in the latest example of investors’ enthusiasm for defense-related bets.

item.title: Japan’s Tax Revenue Rose to Another Record in Latest Fiscal Year
item.description: Japan’s national tax revenue climbed to a record in the fiscal year ended in March as wage hikes, rising employment and earnings on financial assets boosted income tax receipts.

item.title: Switzerland’s Inflation Slows for First Time in Eight Months
item.description: Swiss inflation slowed in June for the first time in eight months, suggesting the impact of lower oil costs are feeding through to the domestic economy.

item.title: India Food Watchdog Targets Energy Drinks Including Red Bull for Misbranding
item.description: India’s food regulator has issued notices to at least six energy-drink makers including Red Bull GmBH’s local unit, for allegedly making misleading claims, tightening oversight of a rapidly expanding category in the country.

item.title: Adani, IHC Sign MOU for $11.5 Billion India Aluminum Project
item.description: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s flagship Adani Enterprises Ltd. and Abu Dhabi’s International Holding Co. plan to invest $11.5 billion in an aluminum project in the eastern state of Odisha, as the Indian conglomerate continues to plow on its metal sector ambitions.

item.title: China’s Private Refiners Snap Up Middle East Oil as Prices Slide
item.description: China’s independent refiners are taking advantage of cheaper Middle Eastern oil after flows accelerated through the Strait of Hormuz, snapping up barrels from producers including Saudi Arabia and Iraq.

item.title: Carlsberg Said to File for India IPO to Raise $700 Million
item.description: Carlsberg A/S confidentially filed draft papers for an initial public offering of its India unit to raise as much as $700 million, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: India Ramps Up Share Sale Plan to Cushion Budget Hit by Oil
item.description: India is fast-tracking efforts to sell stakes in some of its largest state-owned companies, including the nation’s biggest life insurer, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s administration looks to bolster public finances strained by high oil prices.

item.title: Nigeria’s Reforms Revered by Markets Become Political Problem for Tinubu
item.description: Seated beneath a large red canvas umbrella outside Nigeria’s main stock exchange in Lagos, Sulaimon Isiaka watches traders, bankers and executives stream past his newspaper stand each day.

item.title: Yen Climbs With Traders on High Alert for Intervention Risks
item.description: The yen strengthened sharply against the dollar on Thursday amid rising speculation that the currency’s continued weakness may prompt a fresh round of intervention by Japan.

item.title: Novo Owner Backs Italy Fund to Spur Drug Startups Beyond Denmark
item.description: Novo Nordisk A/S’s biggest shareholder is investing in a new Italian startup accelerator, part of an effort to foster early stage life science companies across Europe.

item.title: Congo Plans First Stock Market as AI Mineral Boom Draws Interest
item.description: The Democratic Republic of Congo is drawing up plans for its first stock exchange as it seeks to attract more investment to an economy that’s benefiting from soaring demand for minerals critical to the AI buildout.

item.title: Nvidia Offers Revenue Sharing Model for Aspiring AI Startups
item.description: Nvidia Corp. is looking to expand access to its leading AI hardware by granting token credits to developers in exchange for a share of their future sales.

item.title: RBNZ Won’t Add Member to Policy Committee Ahead of Election
item.description: New Zealand’s central bank will not add a member to its Monetary Policy Committee ahead of the November election, leaving it with three internal and three external members.

item.title: India’s Textile Stocks Become Market Standouts on Trade Deals
item.description: Indian suppliers of T-shirts, bed linen and towels to global retailers such as Walmart Inc. are among this year’s biggest stock-market winners, with some investors betting the rally has further to run.

item.title: OpenAI Proposes Giving the US Government a 5% Stake, FT Says
item.description: OpenAI has begun discussions about giving the US government a 5% stake in the ChatGPT-developer, the Financial Times reported, citing two people familiar with the talks.

item.title: Asia Deals Top $750 Billion Despite Mayhem in Parts of the World
item.description: The world has faced plenty of disruption this year, but that hasn’t derailed business in and out of the Asia Pacific, where the volume of deals such as mergers and acquisitions has already topped $750 billion in 2026.

item.title: Foreign Traders Sell Biggest Pile of Japan Bonds in Three Years
item.description: Overseas investors offloaded the most Japanese bonds in three years in June, official data showed, after a lackluster performance left the debt trailing most of its global peers.

item.title: Warsh Must Beware of Curves Flattening to Deceive
item.description: The bond market is taking him seriously, but that’s not necessarily great news.

item.title: Business Jet Departures in Middle East Drop 30% During Iran War
item.description: Business jet departures in the Middle East declined about 30% in the aftermath of the Iran war, a trend that’s at risk of continuing until a peace deal is signed.

item.title: Japanese Bonds Fall as Inflation Concerns Curb Demand at Auction
item.description: Japanese bonds dropped after demand at a sale of benchmark 10-year notes fell to the lowest since April on growing fiscal concerns and worries the Bank of Japan isn’t raising rates quickly enough to contain inflation.
